violations.text: Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink in the public bathrooms. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/03/2025
Baby formula was observed with a "USE BY DATE" from August 2025. PIC removed formula from the shelf to be sent back to vendor for credits. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/03/2025

The following was observed in poor repair: 1) The meat grip of the slicer was observed to be worn/chipping 2) The hand dryer button in the women's bathroom was observed to not be turning on properly 3) The handle on the sliding door in the prepared foods area was observed to be broken off New Violation. Correct By: 09/17/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the fans in the walk-in coolers were observed with an accumulation of dust. The fans must be cleaned as often as necessary. New Violation. Correct By: 09/03/2025

Rotisserie chickens, which were prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Chicken was observed at 48-49 degrees. Chicken was cooled yesterday. PIC voluntarily disposed of chicken. Proper cooling methods/ how to properly fill out cooling logs with PIC. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2024
Refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair. Per PIC gaskets are in building and are awaiting installation by refrigeration technician. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Slicer was broken down to be washed, rinsed, sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024

Chicken wings and rotisserie chicken, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Person in charge voluntarily disposed of items at the time of inspection. Cooling methods reviewed with PIC and cooling logs left on site. Logs to be reviewed by inspector at next inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024

Various foods were observed held at 46-53 degrees Fahrenheit in the sale endcap display refrigerator. The display refrigerator case was observed maintaining an ambient of 47 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per PIC, various foods were stocked under 3 hours prior to inspection and some were stocked overnight. The items left in the unit overnight was voluntarily disposed of by person in charge. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024

The following was observed in poor repair: 1) The gaskets of the hot-holding unit in the deli area and the refrigerator in the fresh foods area were observed to be peeling off the door. 2) The endcap cooler in the dairy section observed maintaining an ambient temperature of 47 degrees Fahrenheit. Equipment must be in good repair and proper adjustment. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2024

The following was observed of unclean non-food contact surfaces: 1) The fan covers in the walk-in coolers observed with an accumulation of dust 2) The honeycomb vents of the retail display coolers observed with an accumulation of dust 3) The wall in the meat walk-in cooler observed with an accumulation of dust Non-food contact surfaces must be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024

Soap and/or paper towel dispensers were observed next to sinks not meant for hand washing such as three bay sink in bakery and mop sink in back storage area. A sink used for FOOD preparation or UTENSIL washing, or a service sink or curbed cleaning facility used for the disposal of mop water or similar wastes, may not be provided with the handwashing aids and devices required for a HANDWASHING SINK. New Violation. Correct By: 08/17/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (deli slicer, produce slicer, and knives) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/03/2023

The ceiling of a walk-in freezer above stored foods was observed with peeling paint. Condensation was observed accumulating on fans above stored foods in the produce walk-in cooler. Stored foods must be protected from miscellaneous sources of contamination. Produce was moved away from condensation drip and a work order will be placed for peeling paint in walk-in freezer. New Violation. Correct By: 08/17/2023

The following equipment throughout the establishment were observed in poor repair: 1) The handle of a refrigeration unit was broken (cracked and off the screw) in the deli area 2) The gaskets of a refrigeration unit were loose in deli area 3) Light shields observed cracked in deli walk-in cooler 4) Air curtains observed frayed in bakery walk-in freezer New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023

The following equipment throughout the establishment were observed with an accumulation of debris: 1) The fans/fan covers in multiple walk-in coolers had dust build up 2) Light fixture in walk-in cooler had biofilm accumulation 3) Honey comb vents of display coolers in retail area had biofilm 4) Bottom inside area of reach in hot hold unit in deli area had food debris 5) Bottom of storage rack in bakery area had food debris 6) The door tracks of a refrigeration unit in deli area had food debris New Violation. Correct By: 08/17/2023

The sink used for food preparation in the produce area is lacking an air gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. New Violation. Correct By: 08/24/2023

The condensate from plumbing near the ware-washing area in the bakery section was draining into a container. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain. PIC removed container at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/03/2023

The following areas throughout the physical facilities were observed with an accumulation of debris: 1) The wall behind the three bay sink in the deli area had biofilm 2) The walls in the produce walk-in had biofilm New Violation. Correct By: 08/17/2023
